name: Conditions for order
statement_text:
A Runge–Kutta method with elementary weights

                                      Φ : T → R,

has order p if and only if
                          1
                Φ(t) =        , for all t ∈ T such that r(t) ≤ p.              (315a)
                         γ(t)

                                                      1
proof_text:
Proof. The coefficient of F (t)(y0 )hr(t) in (313e) is σ(t) Φ(t), compared with
                                      1
the coefficient in (311d), which is σ(t)γ(t) . Equate these coefficients and we
obtain (315a).

variables.name: Φ
variables.definition: Φ : T → R, the elementary weight function mapping trees to real numbers

variables.name: r(t)
variables.definition: the order (number of vertices) of tree t

variables.name: γ(t)
variables.definition: the density (or symmetry factor) of tree t

equations.content: Taylor expansion of the exact solution
Taylor expansion of the computed (numerical) solution

preamble: The theorem gives conditions for a Runge–Kutta method to have order p, based on comparing Taylor expansions of the exact and numerical solutions. The elementary weight function Φ assigns a real number to each rooted tree t, and γ(t) is the density of the tree.

dependencies.name: Runge Kutta method Taylor expansion formulas
Taylor expansion of exact solution equals numerical method
The Taylor expansion of the exact solution
